summary.one_line: The generated edit was solid mechanically, but the operator's final timeline showed that polish was too conservative and zooms were too sparse.

followed: compared agent output against the operator's final edit
categorized differences into editorial and zoom calibration learnings

issues.title: Visible screen-reading survived the edit
issues.description: The operator cut roughly 55 seconds of narration where the viewer could already read the content on screen.
issues.operator_takeaway: Screen narration is a first-class pacing-drag category for this format.

issues.title: Claude output narration survived the edit
issues.description: The operator removed roughly 50 seconds of narrated LLM recommendations that were interesting to the speaker but low-context for viewers.
issues.operator_takeaway: Keep the reaction; cut most of the narrated output.

issues.title: A list item was misread as a false start
issues.description: The short 'JSON RPC' utterance added distinct information and should have stayed.
issues.operator_takeaway: Not every short preface is a false start; labels and list items can stand on their own.

issues.title: Zoom density was about half the operator's preference
issues.description: The operator used 56 zooms versus 27 suggested zooms, with similar or gentler base scales but much higher frequency.
issues.operator_takeaway: More frequent, section-consistent zooms beat sparse aggressive ones.

recommendations.action: Add explicit pacing-drag subtypes for screen narration, LLM output narration, exploration loops, and transitional bridges.
Raise zoom target density to roughly 3–4 per minute and default to more frequent guide-tier coverage.
Refine false-start rules so short labels/list items are not removed when they add distinct information.
Store operator-adjusted scales, animation choices, and anchor patterns as reusable calibration data.

feedback_hooks.question: Do future diff evals keep showing under-cut screen narration and LLM output narration?
feedback_hooks.how_to_compare: Count false negatives by category across diff sidecars.

feedback_hooks.question: Is the operator consistently preferring grouped, instant, section-consistent zooms?
feedback_hooks.how_to_compare: Compare future zoom sidecars against operator exports.
